response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second, which maps conceptually to the pipeline performance demands of this role. However, his modern C++ exposure is dated and brief (National Instruments, 2014-2015), his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product work, and he has no direct routing, tiling, or navigation-systems experience. He could likely ramp given his systems background and geo-data passion, but he is not an obvious fit for a C++-first routing pipeline team.
strengths: Long-running geospatial platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map) with real production users
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and Linux/DevOps fluency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent roles (2020-present) are primarily TypeScript/Node full-stack, away from systems-level work
